quick_skim.title: Optimizing Teacher-Student Partitioning for Scalable Knowledge Distillation on HPC Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Knowledge Distillation (KD) enables training smaller student models under the guidance of larger teacher models, and the widely adopted TRL library implements it. Yet, TRL treats both models symmetrically, missing opportunities to exploit their pronounced asymmetry in memory footprint, and communication requirements. This paper presents an HPC-aware methodology for KD that decouples teacher and student partitioning efficiently. Our approach achieves up to 67% higher samples-per-second than TRL by avoiding unnecessary teacher-model data structures and selecting the best split strategy. We combine vertical and horizontal partitioning of models, deriving an analytical expression that identifies the existence of inflection points between splitting regimes. These results showed that exploiting teacher--student asymmetry through topology-aware parallelism notably accelerated GKD training on production HPC clusters at our company
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Optimizes teacher-student partitioning to reduce communication overhead in HPC KD
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 优化师生模型划分以减少通信开销，适用于HPC知识蒸馏
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 优化师生模型划分以减少通信开销，适用于HPC知识蒸馏
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 优化师生模型划分以减少通信开销，适用于HPC知识蒸馏
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes HPC-aware teacher-student partitioning for scalable KD, achieving up to 67% higher throughput by exploiting asymmetry in memory and communication.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对知识蒸馏中教师与学生模型不对称的内存和通信需求，提出了一种HPC感知的划分策略。通过垂直和水平划分的组合以及解析表达式找到拐点，该方法避免了不必要的教师模型数据结构，实现了比现有库高67%的采样速率。这项工作为分布式训练中的模型划分提供了可迁移的方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对知识蒸馏中教师与学生模型不对称的内存和通信需求，提出了一种HPC感知的划分策略。通过垂直和水平划分的组合以及解析表达式找到拐点，该方法避免了不必要的教师模型数据结构，实现了比现有库高67%的采样速率。这项工作为分布式训练中的模型划分提供了可迁移的方法。

quick_skim.title: Nautilus: A Verifiable Hierarchical Federated Learning Framework for Vehicular-Edge-Cloud Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative learning for Internet of Vehicles (IoV) scenarios, but the extreme heterogeneity of vehicular-edge-cloud resources severely limits system efficiency. While dynamic scheduling strategies can mitigate this issue, they introduce new trust concerns: how to verify that scheduling decisions are fair, and whether clients faithfully execute optimization instructions without disclosing private data? This paper proposes Nautilus, a verifiable and efficient federated learning framework. First, we design a multi-dimensional resource-aware scheduling algorithm that dynamically allocates compression ratios and training tasks based on vehicles bandwidth, latency, and computing power, significantly improving system training efficiency. Second, to address the trust deficit in the scheduling process, we introduce a Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) mechanism that ensures the fairness of scheduling strategy generation and the compliance of client execution while preserving privacy. Experimental results demonstrate that the framework effectively reduces communication overhead and accelerates model convergence while maintaining system integrity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Federated learning with communication-efficient compression scheduling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 联邦学习中的通信高效压缩调度
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 联邦学习中的通信高效压缩调度
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 联邦学习中的通信高效压缩调度
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Nautilus, a verifiable federated learning framework with a multi-dimensional resource-aware scheduling algorithm that dynamically allocates compression ratios to improve communication efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对车-边-云系统中联邦学习的通信效率和可信问题，提出Nautilus框架，设计多维资源感知调度算法动态分配压缩比率，在保护隐私的同时提升通信效率，实验验证了其有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对车-边-云系统中联邦学习的通信效率和可信问题，提出Nautilus框架，设计多维资源感知调度算法动态分配压缩比率，在保护隐私的同时提升通信效率，实验验证了其有效性。

quick_skim.title: Nautilus: A Verifiable Hierarchical Federated Learning Framework for Vehicular-Edge-Cloud Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative learning for Internet of Vehicles (IoV) scenarios, but the extreme heterogeneity of vehicular-edge-cloud resources severely limits system efficiency. While dynamic scheduling strategies can mitigate this issue, they introduce new trust concerns: how to verify that scheduling decisions are fair, and whether clients faithfully execute optimization instructions without disclosing private data? This paper proposes Nautilus, a verifiable and efficient federated learning framework. First, we design a multi-dimensional resource-aware scheduling algorithm that dynamically allocates compression ratios and training tasks based on vehicles bandwidth, latency, and computing power, significantly improving system training efficiency. Second, to address the trust deficit in the scheduling process, we introduce a Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) mechanism that ensures the fairness of scheduling strategy generation and the compliance of client execution while preserving privacy. Experimental results demonstrate that the framework effectively reduces communication overhead and accelerates model convergence while maintaining system integrity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Resource-aware scheduling with dynamic compression ratio allocation to improve communication efficiency in FL
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过动态分配压缩比的资源感知调度提升联邦学习通信效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过动态分配压缩比的资源感知调度提升联邦学习通信效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过动态分配压缩比的资源感知调度提升联邦学习通信效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Nautilus, a verifiable hierarchical FL framework for vehicular-edge-cloud systems, with multi-dimensional resource-aware scheduling that dynamically allocates compression ratios and training tasks to improve efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对车联网中极端异构的车辆-边缘-云资源，提出了Nautilus可验证联邦学习框架。它设计了多维资源感知调度算法，动态分配压缩比和训练任务，以提升系统效率。框架还通过可验证机制确保调度公平性。实验结果证明了其相对于基线的效率和可验证性。该工作为分布式系统中的通信高效训练提供了实用方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对车联网中极端异构的车辆-边缘-云资源，提出了Nautilus可验证联邦学习框架。它设计了多维资源感知调度算法，动态分配压缩比和训练任务，以提升系统效率。框架还通过可验证机制确保调度公平性。实验结果证明了其相对于基线的效率和可验证性。该工作为分布式系统中的通信高效训练提供了实用方法。
